frozen_question: For one digest-bound open-weight model, one prospectively selected behavior family, and one material-change envelope, do two independently implemented internal methods add held-out predictive or intervention value beyond a tuned behavior-only monitor at acceptable collateral and lifecycle cost?

selection_before_outcomes.model_gate: Choose one open-weight 1B-to-4B parameter causal model with reproducible activation hooks, redistributable evaluation inputs, exact checkpoint and tokenizer digests, and no P2/Q1/Q2 storage displacement.
behavior_candidates: indirect-object or role-binding behavior with exact output scoring
bounded factual-recall relation with counterfactual subjects and exact output scoring
algorithmic comparison behavior with compositional and adversarial transformations
selection_before_outcomes.selection_rule: Use development-only floor/ceiling, positive-control recoverability, and evaluator-sensitivity checks; freeze exactly one candidate and all transformations before sealing the claim-bearing denominator.

method_families.family: sparse feature decomposition or a source-compatible released dictionary
activation patching or path-level causal tracing implemented without METHOD-A labels

comparators: tuned behavior-only monitor with matched access to development labels
linear probe with leakage and selectivity controls
sham, off-target, and dose-matched intervention controls
prospectively frozen combined method if admitted before holdout

data_custody.development_split: method construction and bounded rescue only
data_custody.qualification_split: positive controls, activation checks, label-counterexample review, and evaluator calibration only
data_custody.held_out_split: sealed until every competence gate passes; opened once for the frozen comparison
data_custody.transfer_split: not part of the local claim; opens only after a local result and a separate protocol amendment
data_custody.denominator_separation: No task, label, outcome, retry decision, or tuned configuration may cross from P2-Q1-D1 or ASI-THESEUS-Q2-D2.

competence_gates: Exact model, checkpoint, tokenizer, runtime, capture-site, data, and method identities are frozen.
Both methods recover independently known injected or architectural positive controls at a prospectively set sensitivity floor.
The selected natural behavior is neither floor nor ceiling on development and qualification splits.
The intended capture and intervention paths are active, dose-responsive, and distinguishable from sham and off-target controls.
Blinded label adjudication retains positive examples, hard counterexamples, alternatives, and inter-rater disagreement without treating labels as ground truth.
An independently implemented evaluator detects known prediction and collateral effects without consuming producer labels or held-out outcomes.
Every rescue-ladder choice is exhausted or frozen before the one-time held-out opening, with failures and excluded cases retained.
rescue_ladder: verify capture tensors, token alignment, hook placement, and intervention application
sweep only preregistered method capacity, sparsity, threshold, and regularization ranges on development data
sweep only preregistered intervention sites and doses on development data
check dictionary or path collapse, leakage, inactive controls, evaluator blindness, and task floor or ceiling
use the adjacent model checkpoint only if the original checkpoint fails a predeclared instrumentation gate, then restart with a new campaign identity
stop as implementation-inadequate or instrument-inadequate rather than opening held-out outcomes after a failed competence gate
outcomes: held-out discrimination and calibration beyond the behavior-only baseline
causal effect and uncertainty under matched interventions
feature or path stability across seeds, transformations, and a nearby checkpoint
protected-capability regressions and collateral behavior changes
latency, accelerator time, storage, analyst time, reviewer time, and privacy exposure
net policy decision value under preserve, restrict, escalate, reject, and abstain choices

decision_rule.positive: At least one prospectively frozen internal route improves the primary held-out decision metric over the tuned behavior-only baseline with uncertainty excluding the minimum useful effect, while passing collateral, calibration, stability, and total-cost ceilings.
decision_rule.negative_exact: All competence gates pass, the study is adequately powered for the minimum useful effect, the full rescue ladder is complete, and neither internal route clears the frozen joint benefit/cost rule.
decision_rule.inconclusive: Any competence, power, construct, evaluator, collateral, identity, or resource gate fails after the bounded rescue ladder.
decision_rule.maximum_negative_level: N3 for the exact frozen model, methods, behavior, checkpoint, and evaluator only; field-wide or architecture-general negative language is prohibited.

resource_and_isolation_gate.execution_trigger: Open only after the resource gate, exact model identity, independent evaluator implementation, power analysis, and sealed denominator manifest all pass.

artifact_contract: all producer outputs and evaluator inputs
complete failure, retry, exclusion, and side-effect ledgers
one white-box evidence packet per terminal claim
non_claims: Protocol readiness is not an interpretability experiment or empirical result.
The protocol does not establish that any feature, circuit, label, or intervention is faithful, causal, complete, safe, private, transferable, or useful.
No support, authority, readiness, release, deployment, SOTA, AGI, or ASI movement is authorized.
